description: §65.2.1 — the flagship. Two UNCONDITIONAL component-scope `#{}` rules (`button {}` and `.btn {}`) both provably match one element (`<button class="btn">`) and set the same property (`color`) at the same precedence level → E-STYLE-CONFLICT (hard, error). The styling analog of an exhaustive `match`: the compiler refuses to silently pick a winner. The HARD path is exercised only by a component scope (`@scope`-donut bounded, decidable element set); no soft W-STYLE-CONFLICT-POSSIBLE fires.
rationale: §65.2.1: an UNCONDITIONAL same-property overlap on a provably-shared element, between two rules at the same precedence level, is E-STYLE-CONFLICT. The `tag × class` shape (broad `button` fighting specific `.btn` on a `<button class="btn">`) is the canonical shape (§65.2.4 decidable core). Component scope is decidable → HARD-eligible. E-STYLE-CONFLICT partitions into the fatal errors stream (§65.10 Error row); the soft W-STYLE-CONFLICT-POSSIBLE is NOT emitted for a proven overlap.
